fields.productDescription: Linear helps product and engineering teams manage issues, projects, and roadmaps in one focused workflow.
fields.targetCustomer: Product and engineering teams at software companies
fields.primaryIcpDescription: Software teams that need planning, issue tracking, and roadmap work to stay connected.
icpPains: Planning context splits between issues, projects, and roadmap documents.
Teams lose momentum when product decisions are scattered across tools.
buyingTriggers: The team is standardizing product operations.
Leadership needs roadmap work tied to execution.
icpObjections: Switching tools will slow the team down.
A new planning system may add process overhead.
fields.corePromise: Keep product planning and execution connected in one fast workflow.
keyPromises: Give product teams a focused planning workflow.
commonObjections: Switching tools will slow the team down.
A new planning system may add process overhead.
The team already has a tracker.
forbiddenClaims: Linear replaces every tool in your company

claims.claim: Linear presents itself as a system for issues, projects, and roadmaps.
Linear's product pages emphasize product planning workflows for software teams.

styleReferences.claim: Linear's public site uses concise product language and short declarative headings.

fields.brandPositioning: A focused product development system for modern software teams.
compliance: Do not claim guaranteed business outcomes.
Do not claim full company-wide tool replacement.
fields.conversionPath: Product page to demo or self-serve evaluation

research-voc.summary: Software teams describe planning friction as scattered context and slow handoffs.

claims.claim: Software teams often discuss lost planning context when work moves across issue tracking and roadmap tools.

research-icp.summary: The ICP is product and engineering teams that need shared planning context.

claims.claim: Product and engineering teams need issue tracking and project planning to stay aligned.

research-offer.summary: The offer centers on focused execution across issues, projects, and roadmaps.

claims.claim: Linear groups issue tracking, project planning, and roadmap work into one product system.

synthesize-positioning.summary: Position Linear as the focused product system that keeps planning tied to execution.

claims.claim: The strongest positioning angle is connected planning for product and engineering execution.

synthesize-media-plan.summary: Use LinkedIn for product leadership, Google for intent capture, and Meta for broad awareness testing.

claims.claim: LinkedIn, Google, and Meta are selected as paid channel test surfaces for the launch plan.

research-competitor.summary: Competitors use broad productivity hooks; Linear should counter-position with focused planning.

claims.claim: Competitor hook: "Bring every team into one workspace"

research-keywords.summary: Intent language clusters around roadmap planning, issue tracking, and product management workflows.

claims.claim: Roadmap planning and issue tracking are relevant search-intent themes for product teams.
